Syringa, or Lilac, is a flowering plant known for its fragrant flowers and is a member of the olive family (Oleaceae). 
The flowers of the Lilac plant have been used in traditional medicine for their purported health benefits, including the potential to reduce inflammation and improve mood. 
The bioactive constituents of the Lilac plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. 
The most common herbal preparations made with the Lilac plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
